# gha-repro-kit

## Summary

`gha-repro-kit` is a CLI and GitHub Action for small open source maintainers. It
turns failed GitHub Actions logs into a compact failure packet so maintainers can
understand CI-only failures without reading the full raw log.

## Audience

- Small OSS maintainers
- Contributors debugging CI-only failures
- Bots or agents that need structured CI failure context

## Inputs

- Local GitHub Actions failed log file via `--log-file`
- Public GitHub Actions run via `owner/repo --run RUN_ID`
- Specific failed job in a large workflow via `--job JOB_ID`

## Outputs

- `report.md`: failing job, step, likely command, environment hints, and context
- `summary.md`: short text suitable for PR/issue discussion
- `repro.sh`: local reproduction scaffold when a command can be inferred
- `analysis.json`: structured parser output for automation
- stdout JSON via `--json --no-files` for agents that should not write files

## Current Capabilities

- Parse local log files
- Fetch failed run/job logs with the GitHub CLI
- Support large workflows by analyzing a specific job ID
- Return JSON directly with `--json` / `--format json`
- Skip artifact writes with `--no-files`
- Extract runner, matrix, setup step, and language version hints
- Classify setup-action failures, download/archive failures, and native
  extension build failures
- Preserve public real-world examples in `examples/`

## Non-Goals

- Does not replace CI systems, test runners, or code review bots
- Does not require OpenAI API usage or any AI provider for the MVP
- Does not execute generated reproduction commands automatically
- Does not hide the raw log; it produces a compact handoff next to the raw
  evidence

## Agent Usage

Use this form when an agent needs structured output without creating files:

```bash
gha-repro-kit --log-file ./failed.log --json --no-files
```

The JSON shape is:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "analysis": {},
  "artifacts": null
}
```

When artifacts are desired, omit `--no-files`; `artifacts` contains absolute
paths to `report.md`, `summary.md`, `repro.sh`, and `analysis.json`.

## Open Agent Contribution Task

Issue #1 asks agents and contributors to find public failed GitHub Actions runs
that make good real-world examples.

Goal:

- Add one public failed run to `examples/` with generated artifacts and a short
  `notes.md`.

Preferred inputs:

- `owner/repo`
- failed `RUN_ID`
- failed `JOB_ID` for large workflows
- stack and failure class

Useful failure types:

- `actions/setup-*` failures
- package manager failures
- large workflows that require `--job`
- runner, matrix, or language version mismatches
- Docker or service-container CI-only failures

Expected output:

- `summary.md`, `report.md`, `repro.sh`, and `analysis.json` generated by
  `gha-repro-kit`
- `notes.md` with what the tool got right, what it missed, and the next parser
  improvement
- an `examples/README.md` table entry

Do not include secrets, tokens, private URLs, or personal data. See
`docs/evidence-plan.md` for the exact workflow.

## Agent Metadata

- `agent-card.json`: structured capability metadata for automation
- `docs/agent-integration.md`: agent contract and protocol-readiness notes
- `docs/analysis-json.md`: `analysis.json` field guide
- `.well-known/agent-registration.json`: ERC-8004 discovery registration
  candidate

The project is preparing for agent discovery and hosted-service compatibility.
ERC-8004 discovery metadata is present, but no on-chain registration has been
made yet. x402, ERC-8126, and ERC-8183 are not implemented.

## Important Files

- `src/cli.js`: CLI entry point and artifact writing
- `src/github.js`: GitHub CLI integration
- `src/parser.js`: log parsing, environment inference, failure classification
- `src/report.js`: `report.md`, `summary.md`, and `repro.sh` rendering
- `examples/README.md`: public real-world evidence
- `agent-card.json`: machine-readable capability and safety metadata
- `docs/agent-integration.md`: current agent integration plan
- `docs/analysis-json.md`: structured output contract notes
- `docs/erc-8004-discovery.md`: ERC-8004 discovery preparation notes
- `.well-known/agent-registration.json`: ERC-8004 registration candidate
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: test workflow
- `.github/workflows/notify-discord.yml`: maintainer notification workflow

## Best Next Improvements

- Improve multi-line `Run` step names
- Extract working directory hints
- Add package-manager-specific failure classes
- Generate Dockerfile/devcontainer hints when CI setup is inferable
- Compare failed runs against previous successful runs

## Repository

https://github.com/AZIT1125/gha-repro-kit
